title: chaerophyllum hirsutum subsp. magellense
intro: Chaerophyllum hirsutum subsp. magellense, also known as Chilean lippia or Magellan chaerophyllum, is a plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Andean medicine, where it is valued for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, alleviating menstrual cramps, and supporting respiratory health. Chaerophyllum hirsutum subsp. magellense can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
